---
format: html
title: get_functional_type_classification_path_from_store()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>get_functional_type_classification_path_from_store</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Get Functional-Type Classification Path from Store</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Resolves the newest functional-type classification '.qs' file
for the spatial unit encoded in a 'targets' store path.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>get_functional_type_classification_path_from_store(
  store = targets::tar_path_store(),
  path_spatial_grid = here::here("Data/Input/spatial_grid.csv"),
  path_processed = here::here("Data/Processed/Traits")
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="store">store</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single character string giving the targets store path.
Default: 'targets::tar_path_store()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="path_spatial_grid">path_spatial_grid</code></td>
<td>
<p>Path to the spatial grid CSV catalogue file.
Default: 'here::here(&quot;Data/Input/spatial_grid.csv&quot;)'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="path_processed">path_processed</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single character string giving the directory that contains
dated FT classification '.qs' files.
Default: 'here::here(&quot;Data/Processed/Traits&quot;)'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>The function extracts 'scale_id' from 'store', resolves the owning
'continent_id' from the spatial grid, finds matching FT
classification files in 'path_processed', and returns the most
recent file based on the ISO date suffix in the file name. The
function errors when 'store' is not a spatial store, when no
matching FT classification file exists, or when required inputs
are invalid.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A single character string: the path to the newest matching FT
classification file for the continent that owns the spatial unit.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>get_scale_id_from_store, get_continent_id_from_scale_id,
get_functional_type_classification
</p>


<h3>Examples</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>get_functional_type_classification_path_from_store(
  store = here::here(
    "Data/targets/spatial_regional/eu_r005/",
    "pipeline_spatial_resolution"
  )
)
</code></pre>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
